Pattern to use when formatting the output of the text field expression
FormatPatternExample
Numeric #,##0.###1,234.00
Date MMM d, yyyyJan 01, 2020

Currency $#,##0$1,234

Specify the number of columns in a page
Space between the columns
Width of each column created

Vertical - the filling process run first from top to bottom and then from left to right; the first column is entirely filled, then the second one, the third, etc
Horizontal - the filling process run first from left to right and then from top to bottom; the first row is filled in any column, then the second row, etc.
Allows users to customize the behaviour of the report when there is no data
Reset level for variables that perform calculations

The name of a class that implements the net.sf.jasperreports.engine.fill.JRIncrementerFactory interface to use when creating the incrementer instance for this variable. Incrementers are objects that implement thenet.sf.jasperreports.engine.fill.JRIncrementer interface and handle the incremental calculation performed on the variable's current value with every iteration in the data source.

Definition of the expression that will be used to calculate the initial value of the variable, before any calculations are made.
Example : Suppose to have a variable called variable1 with the expression "new Integer(5)". At every record read will be assign to the variable the integer value 5, so the initial value it isn't important in this context. But suppose to change the expression to "$V{variable1}+5", this means that at every iteration the variable is incremented by 5. In this case an initial value it's necessary otherwise at the first iteration the variable1 is undefined and this breaks all future evaluations.
Name of the style.
Name of the calculation.
Class of the calculation values.
Calulation to perform on the master report variable when returing the value from the subreport
Increment level for variables that perform calculations.

Definition of the expression associated with the calculation.The value of this expression will be calculated at runtime and will represent the value of the corresponding calculation or it will be used in calculation to obtain the value of the calculated variable

Specifies the object position when the report section is affected by stretch.
Specifies the graphic element stretch behavior when the report section is affected by stretch.
Type of the rotation of text object
Name of the mark up language used to embed style information in text content. Supported values are none(Plain text),styled(styled text), rtf(RTF format) and html(HTML format)

The text to be printed is supplied by the associated expression. This expression can be evaluated at a specified moment. This could be useful for example, when we want to have on the first page a text that will be generated only after fetching all the datasource rows
Name of the report level style to use as base style(see <Page styles> element)
Specify group name at which the expression to be executed if the evaluation time is selected as group

Definition of Boolean expression that will return a pattern based on the expression
Example :$F{travel_cost}.equals(1350)? "#,##0.##%":"#,##0.###"

Definition of Boolean expression that will determine if the element should be printed or not
Example : (($F{meet_cancellation_status}.equals("Yes")))
The element will be printed when the record overflows to a new page or a new column
Allows the text field to stretch downwards in order to display all its text when it doesn't fit in the defined text field height
Specifies that the text field should display a blank character instead of null when the text field expression evaluates to null
Name of the group at which the variable is reinitialized, when the Reset Type property is set to Group.
Name of the group at which the variable is incremented, when the Increment Type property is set to Group.
The default report style is used as base style for all the elements that do not explicitly reference a report style definition

If true, tells the report engine to cache the images that are loaded from the same location.

Type of the fill pattern used to fill objects.
Please select a query, whose data will be used to fill the table/crosstab/chart cells.
Select the fields from the query to include in the table, crosstab, or chart. You can modify, add, or remove fields later.
Select the fields of the dataset that will be used to produce table columns. If no fields are selected, the table will be created without any column.

Select the dataset fields that should be used to create table groups. The selected fields will organize and group the table data based on their values. You can modify, add, or remove group fields later. If no fields are selected, the table will be created without grouping.
Horizontal position of the object measured from the left edge of the report band, in pixels.
Vertical position of the object measured from the top edge of the report band, in pixels.
Total horizontal space occupied by the object. This includes all columns and must fit within the band width.
Total vertical space allocated to the object. Content exceeding this height may overflow or stretch based on report settings.
Controls the visibility of the object border around the element.stroke
Defines the thickness of the border line.
Specifies the border line pattern, such as solid, dashed, or dotted.
Sets the color of the border line using a hex or RGB value.
Adds space between the content and the top edge of the element.
Adds space between the content and the left edge of the element.
Adds space between the content and the bottom edge of the element.
Adds space between the content and the right edge of the element.
Background color applied to the table header section.
Background color applied to the column header cells.
Background color applied to the data cells in the table body.
Background color applied to the border of the cells in the table body.
Defines how data is grouped and displayed as columns in the crosstab.
Defines how data is grouped and displayed as rows in the crosstab.
Specifies the numeric fields and aggregation functions (such as sum or count) used to calculate values in the crosstab cells.
Specifies the type of chart to render, such as bar, arc
Field used for the horizontal (category) axis of the chart.
Field used for the vertical (value) axis of the chart.
Field used to assign colors dynamically based on data values or categories.
Field used to display labels on the chart, such as category names or data values.
Specifies how the chart is rendered, for example as a drawn graphic or an image or svg.

Applies a visual theme that controls the chart’s overall style and appearance. Note: After selecting a theme from the dropdown below, please click the Preview button to view the applied changes. Theme updates are not reflected directly on the canvas.
The foreground color of the object, such as font line etc.
The backgournd color of the object
Main heading displayed at the top of the chart, describing what the chart represents
Secondary text displayed below the title, used to add context or explain the data further.
Defines the colors used for each data series in the chart, applied in order to distinguish multiple series visually.
Controls how foreground and background colors are applied, such as transparent or solid.
Specifies the page orientation, such as portrait or landscape, for the report layout.
Defines the overall page margins that control spacing between the content and page edges.
Sets the top margin of the page, determining the space between the top edge and report content.
Sets the bottom margin of the page, determining the space between the bottom edge and report content.
Sets the left margin of the page, controlling horizontal spacing from the left edge.
Sets the right margin of the page, controlling horizontal spacing from the right edge.
Clears the currently defined calculation values and resets them to their default state.
Saves the current calculation configuration for later reuse.
Deletes the selected calculation permanently from the configuration.

Controls the appearance of the top border for the selected element.

Defines the background color behind the image.

Controls how the text is aligned within its container, such as left, center, right, or justified.
Repeats column headers on every new page when the crosstab spans multiple pages.
